question_text: A student makes an electromagnet by wrapping 50 coils of wire around an iron nail connected to a 6V battery. They test it and find it lifts 20 paper clips. They then wrap 100 coils around the same nail with the same battery. Predict how many paper clips the stronger electromagnet will lift and explain your reasoning using two factors that affect electromagnetic strength.
correct_answer: The electromagnet with 100 coils will lift approximately 40 paper clips (double). The two factors are: (1) more coils mean the magnetic field is stronger because each coil contributes to the total field, and (2) the same current flows through more coils, concentrating the magnetism. Doubling the coils roughly doubles the magnetic strength.
explanation: More coils create a stronger magnetic field because each loop of wire adds to the total magnetism, so twice as many coils means roughly twice as much pulling power.
